summary: Remote task-execution agent with server-directed code deployment via wss://api.dalipen.com
details: When the CLI service is started, the package opens a persistent WebSocket to the hardcoded endpoint wss://api.dalipen.com/aigc/task/connect and dispatches incoming `task` frames into subprocess.Popen([sys.executable, <widget_cmd>, '--run', <params_file>,...]), where widget command and parameter contents are supplied by the remote server. This gives the remote server arbitrary command execution on the host running the agent. The service additionally polls getAutoDeployWidget() and, based on server-supplied names/versions/package URLs, downloads zip archives, extracts them, and runs `pip install -r requirements.txt` against the extracted contents with no signature or hash verification, then runs the installed widget as a subprocess — a second server-controlled code-delivery channel. A server-supplied `mihomo` URL is also accepted and applied as the bundled proxy configuration, letting the remote party redirect subsequent outbound traffic through operator-chosen nodes. Separately, ryry/utils.py ships base64-obfuscated Aliyun OSS AccessKey ID/Secret constants (K1/K2) that decode to a live LTAI-prefixed key pair used to sign uploads to p-template-hk.oss-cn-hongkong.aliyuncs.com and p-upload-gz.oss-cn-guangzhou.aliyuncs.com; those credentials belong to the publisher's own OSS buckets.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
